content: SID/STAR procedures are modeled as ordered lists of waypoints/fixes, each optionally carrying altitude restrictions (at/above/below/hard) and speed restrictions (at-or-below). Software represents this as a procedure = ordered list of fix references + per-fix constraints; a flying aircraft on a procedure advances a leg index and applies constraints when compliant, but vectoring is modeled as a separate override on top of (not replacing) the procedure state.
